```{r}
       y     = "Amass",
       title = "TEMP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Rotated Gumbel 270 degree")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_LMA_Amass_TEMP_FOR

```

###LMA-LL
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TEMP_FOR")
lma <- data$`log LMA`
ll <- data$`log LL` 

plot(lma, ll)

cor(lma, ll, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = lma, Y = ll))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) #If TRUE, all rotations of the families in familyset are included (or subtracted). that's why we see a difference between asked and selected
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, #lower tail dependance 'TD'
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) #upper tail dependance 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

# Densités aux pôles (relation négative --> 0.1/0.9)
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.1, selectedCopula) 
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.9, selectedCopula)  

#Plot
#grid construction
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_LMA_LL_TEMP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "LMA",
       y     = "LL",
       title = "TEMP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Frank")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_LMA_LL_TEMP_FOR
```

###LMA-Rmass
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TEMP_FOR")

lma <- data$`log LMA`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(lma, r)

cor(lma, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
u<- pobs(as.matrix(cbind(lma, r)))[,1] 
v<- pobs(as.matrix(cbind(lma, r)))[,2]

#but actually probelem with NA
#problem with NA has to be delt like that:
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = lma, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) #If TRUE, all rotations of the families in familyset are included (or subtracted). that's why we see a difference between asked and selected
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, #lower tail dependance 'TD'
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) #upper tail dependance 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

# Densités aux pôles (relation négative --> 0.1/0.9)
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.9, selectedCopula) 
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.1, selectedCopula)  

#Plot
#grid construction
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_LMA_Rmass_TEMP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "LMA",
       y     = "Rmass",
       title = "TEMP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Gaussian")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_LMA_Rmass_TEMP_FOR
```

###Nmass-Pmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TEMP_FOR")

Nmass <- data$`log Nmass`
lp <- data$`log Pmass` 

plot(Nmass, lp)

cor(Nmass, lp, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Nmass, Y = lp))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) #If TRUE, all rotations of the families in familyset are included (or subtracted). that's why we see a difference between asked and selected
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, #lower tail dependance 'TD'
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) #upper tail dependance 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

# Densités aux pôles (relation négative --> 0.1/0.9)
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.1, selectedCopula) 
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.9, selectedCopula)  

#Plot
#grid construction
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Nmass_Pmass_TEMP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Nmass",
       y     = "Pmass",
       title = "TEMP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Gaussian")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Nmass_Pmass_TEMP_FOR
```

###Nmass-Amass
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TEMP_FOR")

Nmass <- data$`log Nmass`
a <- data$`log Amass` 

plot(Nmass, a)

cor(Nmass, a, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Nmass, Y = a))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) #If TRUE, all rotations of the families in familyset are included (or subtracted). that's why we see a difference between asked and selected
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, #lower tail dependance 'TD'
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) #upper tail dependance 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

# Densités aux pôles (relation négative --> 0.1/0.9)
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.1, selectedCopula) 
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.9, selectedCopula)  

#Plot
#grid construction
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Nmass_Amass_TEMP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Nmass",
       y     = "Amass",
       title = "TEMP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Frank")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Nmass_Amass_TEMP_FOR
```

###Nmass-LL
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TEMP_FOR")

Nmass <- data$`log Nmass`
ll <- data$`log LL` 

plot(Nmass,ll)

cor(Nmass, ll, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Nmass, Y = ll))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1, 2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) 
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, 
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

selectedCopula$family #34 rotated Gumbel copula (270 degrees)
selectedCopula$tau #dépendance négative globale (tau = -0.47)
selectedCopula$par #-1.91 controls overall dependance sterngth must be >= 1 ??
selectedCopula$par2 #0 controls asymettry entre 0 et 1 

# Densités aux pôles
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif

#Plot
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Nmass_LL_TEMP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Nmass",
       y     = "LL",
       title= "TEMP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Frank")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Nmass_LL_TEMP_FOR
```

###Nmass-Rmass
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TEMP_FOR")
Nmass <- data$`log Nmass`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(Nmass,r)

cor(Nmass, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Nmass, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]


#Select copula
families <- c(1, 2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) 
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, 
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

selectedCopula$family #34 rotated Gumbel copula (270 degrees)
selectedCopula$tau #dépendance négative globale (tau = -0.47)
selectedCopula$par #-1.91 controls overall dependance sterngth must be >= 1 ??
selectedCopula$par2 #0 controls asymettry entre 0 et 1 

# Densités aux pôles
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif

#Plot
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Nmass_Rmass_TEMP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Nmass",
       y     = "Rmass",
       title= "TEMP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Gumbel")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Nmass_Rmass_TEMP_FOR
```


###Pmass-Amass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TEMP_FOR")

Pmass <- data$`log Pmass`
a <- data$`log Amass` 

plot(Pmass, a)

cor(Pmass, a, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Pmass, Y = a))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]
```


###Pmass-LL
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TEMP_FOR")

Pmass <- data$`log Pmass`
ll <- data$`log LL` 

plot(Pmass, ll)

cor(Pmass, ll, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
u<- pobs(as.matrix(cbind(Pmass, ll)))[,1] 
v<- pobs(as.matrix(cbind(Pmass, ll)))[,2]

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Pmass, Y = ll))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]
```

###Pmass-Rmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TEMP_FOR")
Pmass <- data$`log Pmass`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(Pmass, r)

cor(Pmass, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Pmass, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

```

###Amass-LL
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TEMP_FOR")
Amass <- data$`log Amass`
ll <- data$`log LL` 

plot(Amass, ll)

cor(Amass, ll, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Amass, Y = ll))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1, 2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) 
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, 
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

selectedCopula$family #34 rotated Gumbel copula (270 degrees)
selectedCopula$tau #dépendance négative globale (tau = -0.47)
selectedCopula$par #-1.91 controls overall dependance sterngth must be >= 1 ??
selectedCopula$par2 #0 controls asymettry entre 0 et 1 

# Densités aux pôles
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif

#Plot
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Amass_LL_TEMP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Amass",
       y     = "LL",
       title= "TEMP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Gaussian")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Amass_LL_TEMP_FOR

```

###Amass-Rmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TEMP_FOR")
Amass <- data$`log Amass`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(Amass, r)

cor(Amass, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Amass, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1, 2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) 
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, 
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

selectedCopula$family #34 rotated Gumbel copula (270 degrees)
selectedCopula$tau #dépendance négative globale (tau = -0.47)
selectedCopula$par #-1.91 controls overall dependance sterngth must be >= 1 ??
selectedCopula$par2 #0 controls asymettry entre 0 et 1 

# Densités aux pôles
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif

#Plot
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Amass_Rmass_TEMP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Amass",
       y     = "Rmass",
       title= "TEMP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Frank")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Amass_Rmass_TEMP_FOR
```



###LL-Rmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TEMP_FOR")
LL <- data$`log LL`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(LL, r)

cor(LL, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = LL, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]


```





##TROP_FOR
###LMA-Nmass

```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")

#LMA - Nmass

lma <- data$`log LMA`
ln <- data$`log Nmass`

plot(lma, ln)

cor(lma, ln, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs") 

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = lma, Y = ln))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) #If TRUE, all rotations of the families in familyset are included (or subtracted). that's why we see a difference between asked and selected
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, #lower tail dependance 'TD'
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) #upper tail dependance 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

# Densités aux pôles (relation négative --> 0.1/0.9)
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif 
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif 

#Plot
#grid construction
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_LMA_Nmass_TROP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "LMA",
       y     = "Nmass",
       title = "TROP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Rotated Gumbel 270 degrees")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_LMA_Nmass_TROP_FOR
```

###LMA - Pmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")

lma <- data$`log LMA`
lp <- data$`log Pmass`

plot(lma, lp)

cor(lma, lp, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = lma, Y = lp))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) #If TRUE, all rotations of the families in familyset are included (or subtracted). that's why we see a difference between asked and selected
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, #lower tail dependance 'TD'
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) #upper tail dependance 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

# Densités aux pôles (relation négative --> 0.1/0.9)
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif 
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif 

#Plot
#grid construction
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_LMA_Pmass_TROP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "LMA",
       y     = "Pmass",
       title = "TROP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Rotated Joe 270 degrees")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_LMA_Pmass_TROP_FOR
```


###LMA - Amass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")

lma <- data$`log LMA`
a <- data$`log Amass` 

plot(lma, a)

cor(lma, a, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs") 

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = lma, Y = a))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]


```

###LMA-LL
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")
lma <- data$`log LMA`
ll <- data$`log LL` 

plot(lma, ll)

cor(lma, ll, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = lma, Y = ll))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]


```

###LMA-Rmass
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")

lma <- data$`log LMA`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(lma, r)

cor(lma, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
u<- pobs(as.matrix(cbind(lma, r)))[,1] 
v<- pobs(as.matrix(cbind(lma, r)))[,2]

#but actually probelem with NA
#problem with NA has to be delt like that:
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = lma, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]


```

###Nmass-Pmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")

Nmass <- data$`log Nmass`
lp <- data$`log Pmass` 

plot(Nmass, lp)

cor(Nmass, lp, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Nmass, Y = lp))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) #If TRUE, all rotations of the families in familyset are included (or subtracted). that's why we see a difference between asked and selected
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, #lower tail dependance 'TD'
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) #upper tail dependance 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

# Densités aux pôles (relation négative --> 0.1/0.9)
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.1, selectedCopula) 
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.9, selectedCopula)  

#Plot
#grid construction
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Nmass_Pmass_TROP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Nmass",
       y     = "Pmass",
       title = "TROP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Survival Joe")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Nmass_Pmass_TROP_FOR
```

###Nmass-Amass
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")

Nmass <- data$`log Nmass`
a <- data$`log Amass` 

plot(Nmass, a)

cor(Nmass, a, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Nmass, Y = a))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) #If TRUE, all rotations of the families in familyset are included (or subtracted). that's why we see a difference between asked and selected
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, #lower tail dependance 'TD'
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) #upper tail dependance 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

# Densités aux pôles (relation négative --> 0.1/0.9)
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.1, selectedCopula) 
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.9, selectedCopula)  

#Plot
#grid construction
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Nmass_Amass_TROP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Nmass",
       y     = "Amass",
       title = "TROP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Frank")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Nmass_Amass_TROP_FOR
```

###Nmass-LL
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")

Nmass <- data$`log Nmass`
ll <- data$`log LL` 

plot(Nmass,ll)

cor(Nmass, ll, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Nmass, Y = ll))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1, 2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) 
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, 
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

selectedCopula$family #34 rotated Gumbel copula (270 degrees)
selectedCopula$tau #dépendance négative globale (tau = -0.47)
selectedCopula$par #-1.91 controls overall dependance sterngth must be >= 1 ??
selectedCopula$par2 #0 controls asymettry entre 0 et 1 

# Densités aux pôles
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif

#Plot
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Nmass_LL_TROP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Nmass",
       y     = "LL",
       title= "TROP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Frank")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Nmass_LL_TROP_FOR
```

###Nmass-Rmass
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")
Nmass <- data$`log Nmass`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(Nmass,r)

cor(Nmass, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Nmass, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]


#Select copula
families <- c(1, 2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) 
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, 
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

selectedCopula$family #34 rotated Gumbel copula (270 degrees)
selectedCopula$tau #dépendance négative globale (tau = -0.47)
selectedCopula$par #-1.91 controls overall dependance sterngth must be >= 1 ??
selectedCopula$par2 #0 controls asymettry entre 0 et 1 

# Densités aux pôles
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif

#Plot
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Nmass_Rmass_TROP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Nmass",
       y     = "Rmass",
       title= "TROP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Gumbel")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Nmass_Rmass_TROP_FOR
```


###Pmass-Amass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")

Pmass <- data$`log Pmass`
a <- data$`log Amass` 

plot(Pmass, a)

cor(Pmass, a, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Pmass, Y = a))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]
```


###Pmass-LL
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")

Pmass <- data$`log Pmass`
ll <- data$`log LL` 

plot(Pmass, ll)

cor(Pmass, ll, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
u<- pobs(as.matrix(cbind(Pmass, ll)))[,1] 
v<- pobs(as.matrix(cbind(Pmass, ll)))[,2]

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Pmass, Y = ll))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]
```

###Pmass-Rmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")
Pmass <- data$`log Pmass`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(Pmass, r)

cor(Pmass, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Pmass, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

```

###Amass-LL
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")
Amass <- data$`log Amass`
ll <- data$`log LL` 

plot(Amass, ll)

cor(Amass, ll, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Amass, Y = ll))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1, 2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) 
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, 
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

selectedCopula$family #34 rotated Gumbel copula (270 degrees)
selectedCopula$tau #dépendance négative globale (tau = -0.47)
selectedCopula$par #-1.91 controls overall dependance sterngth must be >= 1 ??
selectedCopula$par2 #0 controls asymettry entre 0 et 1 

# Densités aux pôles
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif

#Plot
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Amass_LL_TROP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Amass",
       y     = "LL",
       title= "TROP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Gaussian")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Amass_LL_TROP_FOR

```

###Amass-Rmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")
Amass <- data$`log Amass`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(Amass, r)

cor(Amass, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Amass, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1, 2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) 
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, 
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

selectedCopula$family #34 rotated Gumbel copula (270 degrees)
selectedCopula$tau #dépendance négative globale (tau = -0.47)
selectedCopula$par #-1.91 controls overall dependance sterngth must be >= 1 ??
selectedCopula$par2 #0 controls asymettry entre 0 et 1 

# Densités aux pôles
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif

#Plot
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Amass_Rmass_TROP_FOR <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Amass",
       y     = "Rmass",
       title= "TROP_FOR",
       subtitle = paste0("Frank")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Amass_Rmass_TROP_FOR
```



###LL-Rmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_FOR")
LL <- data$`log LL`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(LL, r)

cor(LL, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = LL, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]


```






##TROP_RF
###LMA-Nmass

```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")

#LMA - Nmass

lma <- data$`log LMA`
ln <- data$`log Nmass`

plot(lma, ln)

cor(lma, ln, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs") 

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = lma, Y = ln))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) #If TRUE, all rotations of the families in familyset are included (or subtracted). that's why we see a difference between asked and selected
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, #lower tail dependance 'TD'
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) #upper tail dependance 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

# Densités aux pôles (relation négative --> 0.1/0.9)
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif 
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif 

#Plot
#grid construction
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_LMA_Nmass_TROP_RF <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "LMA",
       y     = "Nmass",
       title = "TROP_RF",
       subtitle = paste0("Gaussian")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_LMA_Nmass_TROP_RF
```

###LMA - Pmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")

lma <- data$`log LMA`
lp <- data$`log Pmass`

plot(lma, lp)

cor(lma, lp, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = lma, Y = lp))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) #If TRUE, all rotations of the families in familyset are included (or subtracted). that's why we see a difference between asked and selected
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, #lower tail dependance 'TD'
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) #upper tail dependance 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

# Densités aux pôles (relation négative --> 0.1/0.9)
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif 
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif 

#Plot
#grid construction
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_LMA_Pmass_TROP_RF <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "LMA",
       y     = "Pmass",
       title = "TROP_RF",
       subtitle = paste0("Rotated Joe 270 degrees")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_LMA_Pmass_TROP_RF
```


###LMA - Amass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")

lma <- data$`log LMA`
a <- data$`log Amass` 

plot(lma, a)

cor(lma, a, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs") 

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = lma, Y = a))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]


```

###LMA-LL
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")
lma <- data$`log LMA`
ll <- data$`log LL` 

plot(lma, ll)

cor(lma, ll, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = lma, Y = ll))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]


```

###LMA-Rmass
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")

lma <- data$`log LMA`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(lma, r)

cor(lma, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
u<- pobs(as.matrix(cbind(lma, r)))[,1] 
v<- pobs(as.matrix(cbind(lma, r)))[,2]

#but actually probelem with NA
#problem with NA has to be delt like that:
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = lma, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]


```

###Nmass-Pmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")

Nmass <- data$`log Nmass`
lp <- data$`log Pmass` 

plot(Nmass, lp)

cor(Nmass, lp, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Nmass, Y = lp))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) #If TRUE, all rotations of the families in familyset are included (or subtracted). that's why we see a difference between asked and selected
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, #lower tail dependance 'TD'
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) #upper tail dependance 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

# Densités aux pôles (relation négative --> 0.1/0.9)
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.1, selectedCopula) 
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.9, selectedCopula)  

#Plot
#grid construction
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Nmass_Pmass_TROP_RF <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Nmass",
       y     = "Pmass",
       title = "TROP_RF",
       subtitle = paste0("Survival Joe")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Nmass_Pmass_TROP_RF
```

###Nmass-Amass
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")

Nmass <- data$`log Nmass`
a <- data$`log Amass` 

plot(Nmass, a)

cor(Nmass, a, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Nmass, Y = a))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) #If TRUE, all rotations of the families in familyset are included (or subtracted). that's why we see a difference between asked and selected
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, #lower tail dependance 'TD'
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) #upper tail dependance 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

# Densités aux pôles (relation négative --> 0.1/0.9)
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.1, selectedCopula) 
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.9, selectedCopula)  

#Plot
#grid construction
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Nmass_Amass_TROP_RF <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Nmass",
       y     = "Amass",
       title = "TROP_RF",
       subtitle = paste0("Frank")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Nmass_Amass_TROP_RF
```

###Nmass-LL
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")

Nmass <- data$`log Nmass`
ll <- data$`log LL` 

plot(Nmass,ll)

cor(Nmass, ll, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Nmass, Y = ll))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1, 2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) 
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, 
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

selectedCopula$family #34 rotated Gumbel copula (270 degrees)
selectedCopula$tau #dépendance négative globale (tau = -0.47)
selectedCopula$par #-1.91 controls overall dependance sterngth must be >= 1 ??
selectedCopula$par2 #0 controls asymettry entre 0 et 1 

# Densités aux pôles
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif

#Plot
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Nmass_LL_TROP_RF <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Nmass",
       y     = "LL",
       title= "TROP_RF",
       subtitle = paste0("Frank")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Nmass_LL_TROP_RF
```

###Nmass-Rmass
```{r}

data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")
Nmass <- data$`log Nmass`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(Nmass,r)

cor(Nmass, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Nmass, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]


#Select copula
families <- c(1, 2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) 
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, 
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

selectedCopula$family #34 rotated Gumbel copula (270 degrees)
selectedCopula$tau #dépendance négative globale (tau = -0.47)
selectedCopula$par #-1.91 controls overall dependance sterngth must be >= 1 ??
selectedCopula$par2 #0 controls asymettry entre 0 et 1 

# Densités aux pôles
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif

#Plot
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Nmass_Rmass_TROP_RF <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Nmass",
       y     = "Rmass",
       title= "TROP_RF",
       subtitle = paste0("Gumbel")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Nmass_Rmass_TROP_RF
```


###Pmass-Amass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")

Pmass <- data$`log Pmass`
a <- data$`log Amass` 

plot(Pmass, a)

cor(Pmass, a, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Pmass, Y = a))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]
```


###Pmass-LL
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")

Pmass <- data$`log Pmass`
ll <- data$`log LL` 

plot(Pmass, ll)

cor(Pmass, ll, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
u<- pobs(as.matrix(cbind(Pmass, ll)))[,1] 
v<- pobs(as.matrix(cbind(Pmass, ll)))[,2]

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Pmass, Y = ll))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]
```

###Pmass-Rmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")
Pmass <- data$`log Pmass`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(Pmass, r)

cor(Pmass, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Pmass, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

```

###Amass-LL
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")
Amass <- data$`log Amass`
ll <- data$`log LL` 

plot(Amass, ll)

cor(Amass, ll, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Amass, Y = ll))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1, 2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) 
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, 
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

selectedCopula$family #34 rotated Gumbel copula (270 degrees)
selectedCopula$tau #dépendance négative globale (tau = -0.47)
selectedCopula$par #-1.91 controls overall dependance sterngth must be >= 1 ??
selectedCopula$par2 #0 controls asymettry entre 0 et 1 

# Densités aux pôles
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif

#Plot
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Amass_LL_TROP_RF <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Amass",
       y     = "LL",
       title= "TROP_RF",
       subtitle = paste0("Gaussian")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Amass_LL_TROP_RF

```

###Amass-Rmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")
Amass <- data$`log Amass`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(Amass, r)

cor(Amass, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = Amass, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1, 2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) 
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, 
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

selectedCopula$family #34 rotated Gumbel copula (270 degrees)
selectedCopula$tau #dépendance négative globale (tau = -0.47)
selectedCopula$par #-1.91 controls overall dependance sterngth must be >= 1 ??
selectedCopula$par2 #0 controls asymettry entre 0 et 1 

# Densités aux pôles
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif

#Plot
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_Amass_Rmass_TROP_RF <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "Amass",
       y     = "Rmass",
       title= "TROP_RF",
       subtitle = paste0("Frank")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
           axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_Amass_Rmass_TROP_RF
```



###LL-Rmass
```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TROP_RF")
LL <- data$`log LL`
r <- data$`log Rdmass` 

plot(LL, r)

cor(LL, r, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs")

#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = LL, Y = r))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]


```


##TROP_RF
###LMA-Nmass

```{r}
data <- data_final %>% filter(BIOME =="TUNDRA")

#LMA - Nmass

lma <- data$`log LMA`
ln <- data$`log Nmass`

plot(lma, ln)

cor(lma, ln, method ='spearman', use= "pairwise.complete.obs") 

#transform in ranks, using pseudo observations 
#pseudo observations are the observations in the [0,1] interval
#problem with NA
df <- na.omit(data.frame(X = lma, Y = ln))
uv <- pobs(as.matrix(df))
u  <- uv[,1]
v  <- uv[,2]

#Select copula
families <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

#fit one by one to see the different AIC per copulas
results <- lapply(families, function(f) {
  fit <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset = f, rotations = T) #If TRUE, all rotations of the families in familyset are included (or subtracted). that's why we see a difference between asked and selected
  data.frame(family_asked = f,
             family_selected = fit$family,
             family_name= fit$familyname,
             BIC = fit$BIC,
             AIC = fit$AIC, 
             tau = fit$tau,
             par1= fit$par,
             par2 = fit$par2,
             lower_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$lower, #lower tail dependance 'TD'
             upper_TD = BiCopPar2TailDep(fit$family, fit$par, fit$par2)$upper) #upper tail dependance 
})

all_result <- do.call(rbind, results)

all_result %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(family_asked, family_selected),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))%>%
  distinct(family_name, .keep_all=T)%>%
  arrange(desc(AIC))

#Best Copula
selectedCopula <- BiCopSelect(u, v, familyset =families, rotations = T, presel = F) #presel if TRUE will pre-select copulas to compare for a faster result. essential to set it to false to test all the families in familyset.
selectedCopula
summary(selectedCopula)

# Densités aux pôles (relation négative --> 0.1/0.9)
BiCopPDF(0.1, 0.9, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle acquisitif 
BiCopPDF(0.9, 0.1, selectedCopula)  # densité au pôle conservatif 

#Plot
#grid construction
u_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
v_seq <- seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 50)
grid  <- expand.grid(u = u_seq, v = v_seq)
grid$density <- BiCopPDF(grid$u, grid$v, selectedCopula)

plot_LMA_Nmass_TROP_RF <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = u, y = v, fill = density)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_contour(aes(z = density), color = "white", 
               alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
     colours = c("white", "#aad576", "#73a942", "#245501", 
                "#1a4301"),
    name    = "Density") +
  labs(x     = "LMA",
       y     = "Nmass",
       title = "TROP_RF",
       subtitle = paste0("Gaussian")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
#useful add aspect.ratio =1 to theme for 1:1 plot
plot_LMA_Nmass_TROP_RF
```



#PLOT BIOME
```{r}
BIOME_plot_LMA_Nmass <- ggarrange(plot_LMA_Nmass_alpine,plot_LMA_Nmass_TEMP_FOR,plot_LMA_Nmass_TROP_FOR,plot_LMA_Nmass_TROP_RF, nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")
BIOME_plot_LMA_Pmass <- ggarrange(plot_LMA_Pmass_TEMP_FOR, plot_LMA_Pmass_TROP_FOR, nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")
BIOME_plot_LMA_Amass <- ggarrange(plot_LMA_Amass_TEMP_FOR, nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")
BIOME_plot_LMA_LL <- ggarrange(plot_LMA_LL_TEMP_FOR, nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")
BIOME_plot_LMA_Rmass <- ggarrange(plot_LMA_Rmass_TEMP_FOR, nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")

BIOME_plot_Nmass_Pmass <- ggarrange(plot_Nmass_Pmass_TEMP_FOR, plot_Nmass_Pmass_TROP_FOR,nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")
BIOME_plot_Nmass_Amass <- ggarrange(plot_Nmass_Amass_TEMP_FOR, nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")
BIOME_plot_Nmass_LL <- ggarrange(plot_Nmass_LL_TEMP_FOR, nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")
BIOME_plot_Nmass_Rmass <- ggarrange(plot_Nmass_Rmass_TEMP_FOR, nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")

BIOME_plot_Pmass_Amass <- ggarrange(, nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")
BIOME_plot_Pmass_LL <- ggarrange(, nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")
BIOME_plot_Pmass_Rmass <- ggarrange(, nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")

BIOME_plot_Amass_LL <- ggarrange(plot_Amass_LL_TEMP_FOR, nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")
BIOME_plot_Amass_Rmass <- ggarrange(plot_Amass_Rmass_TEMP_FOR, nrow=1, common.legend = T, legend= "none")

Figure_all_BIOME <- ggarrange(BIOME_plot_LMA_Nmass,
                           BIOME_plot_LMA_Pmass,
                           BIOME_plot_LMA_Amass,
                           BIOME_plot_LMA_LL,
                           BIOME_plot_LMA_Rmass,
                           BIOME_plot_Nmass_Pmass,
                           BIOME_plot_Nmass_Amass,
                           BIOME_plot_Nmass_LL,
                           BIOME_plot_Nmass_Rmass,
                           BIOME_plot_Pmass_Amass,
                           BIOME_plot_Pmass_LL,
                           BIOME_plot_Amass_LL, ncol=1)
```
